required_header.download_date: ClinicalTrials.gov processed this data on January 2026
required_header.link_text: Link to the current ClinicalTrials.gov record.

clinical_study.brief_title: R-CHOP for Diffuse Large B-Cell Lymphoma Arising as Richter Transformation from Chronic Lymphocytic Leukemia

brief_summary.textblock: This study evaluates R-CHOP (rituximab, cyclophosphamide, doxorubicin, vincristine, prednisone) chemoimmunotherapy in patients with diffuse large B-cell lymphoma (DLBCL) arising as Richter transformation from chronic lymphocytic leukemia (CLL). The study targets patients with GCB-subtype DLBCL, Stage I-II disease (supradiaphragmatic), and IPI score 1 to 3. Patients with underlying CLL on prior watch-and-wait (no prior CLL-directed therapy) are the primary population. The primary endpoint is complete response rate by PET/CT at end of treatment.

detailed_description.textblock: Patients with biopsy-confirmed DLBCL as Richter transformation from CLL (requiring documented prior CLL diagnosis) are eligible. GCB subtype by Hans algorithm is required. Underlying CLL must not have been treated with cytotoxic chemotherapy or BTK inhibitors prior to transformation. Patients receive R-CHOP every 21 days for 6 planned cycles. Interim PET/CT is performed after cycle 3 to assess early response (Deauville scoring). Patients with dual expressor (BCL2+/MYC protein+) but no MYC rearrangement by FISH are eligible. Tumor lysis syndrome prophylaxis with allopurinol is required. Patients with stable gout managed with allopurinol benefit from dual-purpose prophylaxis.

clinical_study.study_design: Intervention Model: Single Group Assignment, Primary Purpose: Treatment

criteria.textblock:
Inclusion Criteria:
- Biopsy-confirmed diffuse large B-cell lymphoma (DLBCL), germinal center B-cell (GCB) subtype by Hans algorithm immunohistochemistry
- Documented history of chronic lymphocytic leukemia (CLL) prior to DLBCL diagnosis, consistent with Richter transformation
- CLL previously managed with observation (watch-and-wait) only; no prior CLL-directed cytotoxic chemotherapy or BTK inhibitor therapy
- CLL immunophenotype: CD5+, CD23+, CD20+ (dim) on flow cytometry
- Ann Arbor Stage I or II (supradiaphragmatic disease only)
- International Prognostic Index (IPI) score 1 to 3
- CD20-positive DLBCL by IHC (CD20 strongly positive)
- MYC rearrangement negative by FISH (dual expressor by IHC acceptable if FISH negative for double-hit)
- Elevated LDH at diagnosis
- Age 50 to 75 years
- ECOG performance status 0 or 1
- Adequate organ function: ANC greater than or equal to 1.0 (or explained by underlying CLL lymphocytosis), platelets greater than or equal to 75, creatinine less than or equal to 1.5x ULN, AST/ALT less than or equal to 2x ULN, ejection fraction greater than or equal to 50% (required prior to doxorubicin)
Exclusion Criteria:
- Non-GCB (activated B-cell, ABC) subtype DLBCL by Hans algorithm
- Double-hit or triple-hit lymphoma: concurrent MYC rearrangement plus BCL2 and/or BCL6 rearrangement by FISH
- Stage III or IV disease (infradiaphragmatic or disseminated disease)
- Prior CLL therapy with BTK inhibitors (ibrutinib, acalabrutinib) or venetoclax
- Prior rituximab or anti-CD20 monoclonal antibody therapy
- Large cell lymphoma involving the bone marrow (CLL marrow involvement acceptable)
- Active central nervous system lymphoma
- Left ventricular ejection fraction less than 50% (contraindication to doxorubicin)
- Uncontrolled active infection
- Prior allogeneic stem cell transplant
